macro_summary: Fast-moving, conflicting geopolitics overnight: Business Recorder reports oil rose after Iran shut the Strait of Hormuz again and Trump threatened new attacks, yet other wires the same morning say US-Iran talks concluded with mediators citing progress and Asian stocks rallying on that progress -- genuine uncertainty, not a settled direction. Domestically, the government's unilateral fuel-price cut has the local oil industry (OMCs/refiners) publicly objecting over margin pressure (Dawn Business). Select Technologies IPO book-building formally opened today at PSX -- a live, material catalyst for AIRLINK.

OGDC.summary: Iran reportedly shut Hormuz again and Trump threatened new strikes, which would lift crude and help E&P realised prices -- but same-morning wires say talks concluded with progress cited and Asian markets rallying, so the situation is genuinely unresolved. Mild positive tilt on the supply-risk headline, tempered by the conflicting de-escalation signal.
headlines: Oil rises after Iran shuts Hormuz again, Trump threatens new attacks
US and Iran conclude high-level talks in Switzerland, mediators say

PPL.summary: Same Hormuz-reescalation-vs-talks-progress tension applies to PPL's gas/oil realisations as OGDC -- mild positive tilt, medium confidence given the conflicting same-day signals.
headlines: Oil rises after Iran shuts Hormuz again, Trump threatens new attacks
Attock Refinery to Sell Hospital Stake to Own Chairman

PSO.summary: Double headwind for an OMC: the local oil industry is publicly objecting to the government's unilateral fuel-price cut squeezing margins, and a renewed Hormuz supply scare raises input-cost risk on top of that -- compounding negative, though the geopolitical leg is still fluid.
headlines: Oil industry cries out over unilateral cut in fuel prices
Oil rises after Iran shuts Hormuz again, Trump threatens new attacks

PRL.summary: Refiner feedstock-cost risk rises if Hormuz tension persists, and the domestic margin-squeeze complaint over the unilateral price cut applies to refiners too -- a related-party hospital-stake sale by sister company Attock Refinery is a minor governance note, not a direct driver.
headlines: Oil industry cries out over unilateral cut in fuel prices
Attock Refinery to Sell Hospital Stake to Own Chairman

ATRL.summary: Attock Refinery itself is selling a hospital stake to its own chairman -- a related-party transaction that's a minor governance optics concern rather than an operational catalyst; low-confidence mild negative.
headlines: Attock Refinery to Sell Hospital Stake to Own Chairman

NRL.summary: Same Attock-group related-party hospital-stake sale as ATRL -- minor governance note for the wider Attock group, low-confidence mild negative.
headlines: Attock Refinery to Sell Hospital Stake to Own Chairman

AIRLINK.summary: Select Technologies IPO book-building formally opened today at PSX, targeting up to Rs3.7bn -- a live, confirmed catalyst (not just scheduled) directly relevant to AIRLINK's tech-IPO exposure.
headlines: Select Technologies IPO book-building opens today, company eyes up to Rs3.7bn

CPHL.summary: Citi Pharma is spinning off its real estate assets into a REIT -- a value-unlocking corporate action, mild positive.
headlines: Citi Pharma to spin off real estate assets into a REIT

WAVES.summary: Waves is resuming air-conditioner production in Pakistan after nearly four years -- a genuine operational restart, mild positive.
headlines: After nearly four years, Waves to resume air conditioner production in Pakistan
